payload.answer: There is a conflict: the existing memory says Sara is your daughter, while the new input says Sara is your niece. I can’t treat both as the current fact about the same person unless you choose how to resolve it. Safe actions available: approve_supersession to replace the existing daughter relationship with the new niece relationship; keep_both if these refer to different people or both should be retained with ambiguity; reject_new to keep the existing memory unchanged and not store the new statement; edit to clarify or correct the wording before saving.

entity_resolution.reason: The existing memory and new input both refer to Sara, but they describe conflicting family relationships: daughter vs niece. It is ambiguous whether this is the same Sara with corrected information or two different people named Sara.

details: Conflict requires user choice before committing the new relationship.
